title: ophioglossum regulare
intro:
Ophioglossum regulare, also known as the resurrection fern or resurrection plant, is a type of rare and unique plant species that belongs to the Ophioglossaceae family, characterized by its ability to revive and regrow its fronds after prolonged periods of desiccation.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ophioglossum regulare include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. 

Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the dried fronds in hot water or by using them in the form of a tincture or decoction, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
